mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 7.0%, labor market deterioration signals impending recessionary conditions, causing equity valuations to contract over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 7.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and stable consumer price inflation indicate robust consumer demand without overheating, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in >60% of occurrences, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and earnings growth, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below 0.5 over any 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP confirms sustained economic expansion, supporting corporate earnings trajectories and producing positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero correlation with 60-day forward returns, or the signal yields negative alpha in >50% of quarters over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly inflation spikes trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally following inflation spikes >0.4% MoM, or the negative return signal fails to achieve a t-statistic magnitude greater than 1.5 in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained declines in housing starts signal weakening residential investment and broader credit contraction, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: Housing start declines fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with the signal showing positive mean returns in >60% of triggered instances over a rolling 3-year evaluation.
